Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient registered on the DigiLab Connect platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Patient entity.

email.description: The patient's email address, used for communication and login.

gender.description: The patient's gender (e.g., Male, Female, Other).

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the patient's profile image.

locationAddress.description: The patient's primary residential address, used for home sample collection.

country.description: The country where the patient resides.

cityTown.description: The city or town where the patient resides.

allowLocationAccess.description: Indicates if the patient has granted permission for location access (for services like finding nearby labs).

registrationDateTime.description: The date and time when the patient registered on the platform.

TestType.title: TestType
TestType.description: Defines a type of medical test available through DigiLab Connect.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TestType entity.

name.description: The name of the medical test (e.g., 'Complete Blood Count', 'Urine Analysis').

description.description: A detailed description of the test, including what it measures and its purpose.

price.description: The standard price for this test.

minAge.description: Minimum age required or recommended for this test.

maxAge.description: Maximum age required or recommended for this test.

preparationInstructions.description: Instructions for the patient to prepare for the test (e.g., fasting, medication restrictions).

availableLabIds.description: References to Labs where this test can be performed. (Relationship: TestType N:N Lab)

Lab.title: Lab
Lab.description: Represents a laboratory facility associated with DigiLab Connect for sample analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lab entity.

name.description: The official name of the laboratory.

address.description: The physical address of the laboratory.

cityTown.description: The city or town where the laboratory is located.

country.description: The country where the laboratory is located.

phoneNumber.description: The main contact phone number for the laboratory.

email.description: The main contact email for the laboratory.

operatingHours.description: A description of the laboratory's operating hours (e.g., 'Mon-Fri 9AM-5PM').

description.description: A brief description of the lab's services or specialties.

ownerId.description: Reference to the ID of the user who owns or manages this lab. (Relationship: LabOwner 1:N Lab)

testTypeIds.description: References to TestTypes that this lab is equipped to perform. (Relationship: Lab N:N TestType)

LabPersonnel.title: LabPersonnel
LabPersonnel.description: Represents a certified laboratory personnel capable of collecting samples and submitting results.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LabPersonnel entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the lab personnel.

lastName.description: The last name of the lab personnel.

email.description: The email address of the lab personnel, used for communication and login.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the lab personnel.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the lab personnel's profile image.

certificationDetails.description: Details about the personnel's certifications and licenses.

isFreelancer.description: Indicates if the personnel operates as a freelancer on the platform.

associatedLabIds.description: References to Labs with which this personnel is affiliated. (Relationship: LabPersonnel N:N Lab)

registrationDateTime.description: The date and time when the lab personnel registered on the platform.

averageRating.description: The average rating received by this personnel from patients.

status.description: The current status of the lab personnel (e.g., 'active', 'on_leave', 'inactive').

TestRequest.title: TestRequest
TestRequest.description: Represents a patient's request for a medical test, including collection details and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TestRequest entity.

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient who made the request. (Relationship: Patient 1:N TestRequest)

testTypeId.description: Reference to the TestType requested by the patient. (Relationship: TestType 1:N TestRequest)

symptoms.description: A description of the patient's symptoms relevant to the test.

collectionMethod.description: The chosen method for sample collection (e.g., 'home_collection', 'lab_visit').

collectionAddress.description: The specific address for home sample collection, if applicable.

collectionLatitude.description: The latitude of the collection location for geo-tagging.

collectionLongitude.description: The longitude of the collection location for geo-tagging.

scheduledCollectionDateTime.description: The agreed-upon date and time for sample collection.

preferredLabId.description: Optional reference to a specific Lab preferred by the patient. (Relationship: Lab 1:N TestRequest)

requestStatus.description: The current status of the test request (e.g., 'Pending', 'Allocated', 'Sample Collected', 'Analysis In Progress', 'Results Uploaded', 'Payment Pending', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

allocatedPersonnelId.description: Reference to the LabPersonnel allocated to this request. (Relationship: LabPersonnel 1:N TestRequest)

creationDateTime.description: The date and time when the test request was created.

lastUpdateDateTime.description: The date and time when the test request was last updated.

supportingFileUrls.description: URLs to any supporting files uploaded with the request (e.g., doctor's prescription).

paymentTransactionId.description: Reference to the PaymentTransaction associated with this request. (Relationship: PaymentTransaction 1:1 TestRequest)

PaymentTransaction.title: PaymentTransaction
PaymentTransaction.description: Records details of a payment made for a test request.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentTransaction entity.

testRequestId.description: Reference to the TestRequest associated with this payment. (Relationship: TestRequest 1:1 PaymentTransaction)

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient who made the payment. (Relationship: Patient 1:N PaymentTransaction)

amount.description: The amount paid for the test request.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for payment (e.g., 'Mpesa', 'Bank Transfer').

mobileNumber.description: The mobile number used for mobile payments (e.g., Mpesa).

accountNumberUsed.description: The account number used for alternative payment methods (e.g., bank transfer).

transactionStatus.description: The current status of the payment transaction (e.g., 'Pending', 'Successful', 'Failed', 'Refunded').

transactionDateTime.description: The date and time when the payment transaction occurred.

externalTransactionId.description: The unique transaction ID provided by the external payment gateway (e.g., Mpesa transaction code).

paymentLink.description: A URL for initiating an external payment process.

confirmationCode.description: A code provided by the user to confirm manual payments.

TestResult.title: TestResult
TestResult.description: Stores the results of a medical test for a patient.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TestResult entity.

testRequestId.description: Reference to the TestRequest this result fulfills. (Relationship: TestRequest 1:1 TestResult)

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient whose test results these are. (Relationship: Patient 1:N TestResult)

testTypeId.description: Reference to the TestType that was performed. (Relationship: TestType 1:N TestResult)

labId.description: Reference to the Lab where the analysis was conducted. (Relationship: Lab 1:N TestResult)

labPersonnelId.description: Reference to the LabPersonnel who uploaded these results. (Relationship: LabPersonnel 1:N TestResult)

resultDetails.description: The actual detailed results of the test, potentially in a structured (JSON string) or plain text format.

resultFileUrl.description: URL to the downloadable PDF or image file of the official test report.

submissionDateTime.description: The date and time when the test results were submitted.

aiInsights.description: AI-generated insights or analysis based on the test results and patient history.

isPrinted.description: Indicates if the result has been printed by the patient.

isDownloaded.description: Indicates if the result has been downloaded by the patient.

PersonnelRating.title: PersonnelRating
PersonnelRating.description: Records patient feedback and ratings for a lab personnel after a test request is completed.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PersonnelRating entity.

testRequestId.description: Reference to the TestRequest for which the rating is given. (Relationship: TestRequest 1:N PersonnelRating)

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient who submitted the rating. (Relationship: Patient 1:N PersonnelRating)

labPersonnelId.description: Reference to the LabPersonnel being rated. (Relationship: LabPersonnel 1:N PersonnelRating)

ratingScore.description: The numerical rating given (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: Optional text comment provided by the patient.

ratingDateTime.description: The date and time when the rating was submitted.

AbuseReport.title: AbuseReport
AbuseReport.description: Records reports of misconduct or abuse by patients against lab personnel.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AbuseReport entity.

testRequestId.description: Optional reference to the TestRequest related to the reported incident. (Relationship: TestRequest 1:N AbuseReport)

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient who submitted the report. (Relationship: Patient 1:N AbuseReport)

labPersonnelId.description: Reference to the LabPersonnel against whom the report is made. (Relationship: LabPersonnel 1:N AbuseReport)

reportReason.description: A brief reason or category for the report (e.g., 'Delay', 'Misconduct', 'Unprofessionalism').

reportDescription.description: A detailed description of the reported incident.

reportDateTime.description: The date and time when the report was submitted.

reportStatus.description: The current processing status of the report (e.g., 'Pending Review', 'Under Investigation', 'Resolved', 'Dismissed').

definition.description: Stores individual patient profiles. The document ID (`{patientId}`) MUST be the Firebase Authentication UID of the patient. This forms the root for all patient-specific data, enabling path-based authorization. Includes user profile details.

Stores definitions of all available medical tests. This collection is publicly readable to allow patients and personnel to browse test options. The document ID (`{testTypeId}`) corresponds to the unique ID of the test type.

params.name: testTypeId
params.description: Unique identifier for the TestType document.

definition.description: Contains details of laboratory facilities. This collection is publicly readable for patients to find labs. Write access is restricted to the specific `ownerId` denormalized within the document, ensuring authorization independence for lab management. The document ID (`{labId}`) corresponds to the unique ID of the lab.

params.name: labId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Lab document.

definition.description: Stores profiles for certified laboratory personnel. This collection is publicly readable for patients to view personnel details and for system allocation. The document ID (`{personnelId}`) MUST be the Firebase Authentication UID of the lab personnel, enabling self-management of their profile. Includes personnel certifications and status.

params.name: personnelId
params.description: Unique identifier for the LabPersonnel document (Firebase Auth UID).

definition.description: A dedicated collection to grant 'Lab Owner' role access. The document ID (`{ownerId}`) MUST be the Firebase Authentication UID of the user, signifying their role. The document itself can be minimal, its existence grants the role. This collection ensures DBAC (Database-Based Access Control) for 'Lab Owner' privileges without custom claims.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Lab Owner (Firebase Auth UID).

definition.description: Stores individual test requests made by a patient. This subcollection ensures private access, as `{patientId}` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. The document also includes denormalized `patientId` and `allocatedPersonnelId` for authorization independence and simplified rules for both patients and assigned personnel. The `requestStatus` field explicitly models the request lifecycle.

params.name: patientId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Patient who owns this request (Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: testRequestId
params.description: Unique identifier for the TestRequest document.

definition.description: Records payment details for a patient's test request. Located as a subcollection of the patient, ensuring private access. Includes denormalized `patientId` and `testRequestId` for authorization independence and direct linking to the associated test request.

params.name: patientId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Patient who made the payment (Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: transactionId
params.description: Unique identifier for the PaymentTransaction document.

definition.description: Stores the results of a medical test for a patient. This subcollection provides private access for the patient. Includes denormalized `patientId`, `testRequestId`, `labId`, and `labPersonnelId` for authorization independence, allowing both the patient and the responsible lab personnel to access relevant results. Supports AI insights, printing, and downloading features.

params.name: patientId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Patient whose results these are (Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: resultId
params.description: Unique identifier for the TestResult document.

definition.description: Records patient feedback and ratings for a lab personnel. This subcollection ensures private access to the rating by the submitting patient. Includes denormalized `patientId`, `labPersonnelId`, and `testRequestId` for authorization independence and clear context.

params.name: patientId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Patient who submitted the rating (Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: ratingId
params.description: Unique identifier for the PersonnelRating document.

definition.description: Stores reports of misconduct or abuse by patients against lab personnel. This subcollection ensures private access to the report by the submitting patient. Includes denormalized `patientId`, `labPersonnelId`, and optional `testRequestId` for authorization independence and contextual linking. The `reportStatus` field explicitly models the report's processing state.

params.name: patientId
params.description: Unique identifier for the Patient who submitted the report (Firebase Auth UID).

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for the AbuseReport document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for DigiLab Connect prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy revolves around **Authorization Independence** through extensive denormalization and **Structural Segregation** to ensure homogeneous security postures for collections.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design completely eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check authorization. For all patient-specific data (TestRequest, PaymentTransaction, TestResult, PersonnelRating, AbuseReport), the `patientId` field is denormalized directly into each document. This means a patient's access to their data can be verified simply by `request.auth.uid == resource.data.patientId`. Similarly, for `TestResult` and `PersonnelRating`, the `labPersonnelId` is denormalized, allowing `LabPersonnel` to access relevant documents where they are involved without needing to traverse parent collections or perform lookups. For `Lab` entities, the `ownerId` is present on the `lab` document itself, and a dedicated `/lab_owners/{ownerId}` collection provides a simple, direct DBAC role check based on `request.auth.uid` matching the document ID.

This denormalization is critical for several reasons:
1.  **Atomic Operations:** Transactions and batch writes involving related documents become inherently secure and don't risk inconsistencies due to `get()` calls that might fetch stale data or fail. For instance, creating a `TestRequest` and its associated `PaymentTransaction` can be done atomically, with both documents immediately containing the necessary `patientId` for authorization.
2.  **Simplified Rules:** Rules become concise and explicit, directly checking fields on `request.resource.data`. This drastically reduces complexity and potential for errors compared to rules that rely on hierarchical dependencies.
3.  **Performance:** No `get()` calls in rules mean faster rule evaluation and no additional read operations for authorization checks, leading to better overall performance and reduced costs.

**Structural Segregation (QAPs - Rules are not Filters):**
Collections are designed with a homogeneous security posture, making `list` operations (QAPs - Query as Authorization Primitives) secure by default:
*   **`/patients/{patientId}`:** This is the root for all private, patient-specific data. Subcollections like `/patients/{patientId}/test_requests`, `/patients/{patientId}/test_results`, etc., are secured by requiring `request.auth.uid` to match `{patientId}` in the path. This ensures that a patient can only `list` *their own* requests, results, etc., directly from their personalized subcollection.
*   **`/test_types` and `/labs`:** These are top-level collections designed for public or broadly accessible data. They can be freely `list`ed by any authenticated user (patients, personnel) to discover available tests and labs. Specific actions (like creating/updating `Lab` documents) would be restricted to `LabOwner`s using the `ownerId` on the `Lab` document.
*   **`/lab_personnel`:** This collection can be `list`ed by patients to find available personnel. Individual `LabPersonnel` can also manage their own profile, with `request.auth.uid` matching their `personnelId`.
*   **`/lab_owners/{ownerId}`:** This acts as a DBAC role collection. Existence of a document at `/lab_owners/request.auth.uid` grants owner permissions.

This clear separation allows for simple `allow read: if request.auth.uid == userId` patterns for private data and `allow read: if request.auth != null` for public data, ensuring queries do not return unauthorized documents.
